column1: To automate the testing of the injector module
Injector is a module made in 2 variants and can be started in 2 different ways:
The logged module requires the following configs:
A test is a function in PowerShell v2.0 that tests one simple condition,
and returns a boolean value indicating that the condition is met.
Successful execution of the test corresponds to a logical truth.
The Message property must correspond to the test.
In case of any test outcome, the diagnostics confirming this outcome is written to this property.
For example, if the log contains the string being searched for (by which the test condition is checked),
the found string is copied into this property.
If the string is missing, the field is filled with an arbitrary
description of the reason (for example, "*substring*: no match in c:/temp/logname.log").
The following tests should be written:
* these three tests should be done last
The output should be a script in PowerShell v2.0.
The script should return a return code to the OS equal to the number of failed tests.
On startup, the script should perform an auto-update similar to other autotest scripts.
At the beginning of each test (except for *proxy* tests and other specified cases), the script should run the load file from the current directory.
launch the loader.exe file from the current directory, and after the test is completed, kill the process and delete the log files.
The test should log the following
If any test fails (except gasket tests), further tests should be skipped,
and the script should be terminated.
Skipped tests are logically counted as failed tests.
Settings are placed in variables, placed in the very beginning of the script:
- module warmup timeout (20 seconds by default)
- browsers warm-up timeout (35 seconds by default)
- browser persistence timeout (1 minute by default)
From sinj config the first pad is selected, TCP-connection is opened.
The test is successful if the TCP connection was opened.
The first proxy is selected from the dinj configuration and a TCP connection is opened on the specified port.
The test is successful if the TCP connection was opened.
The dpost config tests all pads by opening a TCP connection on a given port.
The test is successful if it was possible to open a TCP connection to at least one pad.
The test is successful if after the warm-up time:
- the file loader.log appears on the required path
- there is an entry in it
After the warm-up time the Chrome process is started.
After the browser warming up the following conditions are checked:
- core-dll.log file appears in the right path
(this is the message for the test)
- there are at least 3 chrome.exe processes in the list of processes after browser stability timeout
(if this item is violated, the test is failed, the following line is added to the test message
- if there is an entry in the log
then the test fails, and this message and the addresses of the functions (below in the log) are copied to the test message.
After the warm-up time the Mozilla Firefox process is started.
After the browser warm-up the following conditions are checked:
- core-dll.log file appears on the necessary path
(this is a message for the test)
- there is at least 1 process firefox.exe in the list of processes after the browser stability timeout
(if this item is violated, the test is failed, the following line is added to the test message
After the warm-up time, the Internet Explorer process is started.
After the browser warm-up the following conditions are checked:
- core-dll.log file appears in the right path
(this is the message for the test.
If there is more than one line with version, glue all of them to the message)
- there is at least 1 process iexplore.exe in the list of processes after the browser stability timeout
(if this item is violated the test is failed, the following line is added to the test message
After a warm-up time, the Chrome process is started.
After the browser warm-up time, the following conditions are checked:
- at least one chrome.exe process has the command line keys
After the warm-up time, the file %APPDATA%\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\<profileName>\prefs.js is searched for.
The test is considered successful if there is a substring in the file
After the warm-up time the value of the registry key is checked:
* automatic preparation of VMs for testing, includes:
- add tests that check for injects in the browser WITHOUT parsing logs.
To do this, we need to find a way to view the page code of the running browser, from the script.
* basic inject test (common for all browsers)
After a warm-up time, the browser is launched.
After the warm-up time a page from the list is opened.
After the page is loaded, its source code is opened.
A substring is searched for in the source code.
The test is successful if the substring is found.
The test requires working with windows via WinAPI.
